backlog.title: Bug Fix Sprint: Discovery Edge Cases
backlog.description: Fix 6 issues (1 Major, 5 Minor) found during creative end-to-end QA of the weave implementation. Issue 1 (Major) silently breaks a PRD-supported multi-entry package shape and cascades into spurious errors. Issues 2-6 are edge cases and footguns in JSDoc extraction, root-level index.ts handling, symlinked store paths, check validation, and node_modules/hidden-file discovery. All PRD §13 acceptance gates currently pass; these fixes close the remaining gaps without changing any public API.

milestones.title: Multi-Entry Package Resolution (Issue 1 — Major)
milestones.description: Fix classifyDir case (a) to iterate ALL pi.extensions entries and select the first existing one, instead of only checking entries[0]. This is the root cause of the init-vs-discovery inconsistency (extdir.hasPiExtensions already iterates correctly) and the upstream cause of spurious duplicate-relTag errors and mis-discovered stray entries. Mirrors PRD §7.1 'the first existing pi.extensions entry'.

tasks.title: Fix classifyDir to iterate all pi.extensions entries
tasks.description: In internal/discover/discover.go classifyDir case (a), replace the entries[0]-only existence check with a loop over all entries that selects the first existing one. Only fall through if NO entry exists. This unifies discovery with extdir.hasPiExtensions and resolves the PRD-supported multi-entry package shape.

subtasks.title: Iterate pi.extensions entries in classifyDir and select first existing
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_cascade_map.md Bug 1 and architecture/fix_design.md §Bug 1. classifyDir case (a) in internal/discover/discover.go (~line 136-148) only stat-checks entries[0] via `entryFile := filepath.Join(path, entries[0]); if fileExists(entryFile)`. extdir.hasPiExtensions (extdir.go:393-397) correctly iterates ALL entries and returns true if ANY exists. The fix mirrors that iteration: loop over entries, break on the first fileExists hit. This also resolves the init-vs-discovery inconsistency noted in the PRD aggravating factor (extdir.HasExtensionEntry used by `weave init` already iterates all entries).
2. INPUT: `pkg PackageJSON` from ParsePackageJSON (already called at the top of classifyDir at ~line 132). `entries := toStringSlice(pkg.Pi.Extensions)` (~line 137) yields []string of declared pi.extensions paths relative to `path`. `path` is the directory being classified. `root` is the absolute extensions dir. `hasPkg` and `perr` are already in scope.
3. LOGIC: Replace the single-entry check:
   ```go
   entryFile := filepath.Join(path, entries[0])
   if fileExists(entryFile) { ... }
   ```
   with a loop:
   ```go
   var entryFile string
   for _, e := range entries {
       if f := filepath.Join(path, e); fileExists(f) {
           entryFile = f
           break
       }
   }
   if entryFile != "" {
       relTag := relTagForDir(root, path)
       jsdoc := ExtractJSDoc(entryFile)
       ext := BuildExtension(path, entryFile, relTag, "package", pkg, hasPkg, jsdoc)
       return &ext, true, false
   }
   // no existing entry among any declared → fall through to b/c/d/e
   ```
   Implement PRD §7.1 'the first existing pi.extensions entry'. No external services to mock. The existing fall-through comment ('pi.extensions names a non-existent file → NOT a package; fall through') should be updated to reflect 'no existing entry among ANY declared pi.extensions → fall through'.
4. OUTPUT: classifyDir returns (ext, true, false) for packages with >=1 existing entry regardless of the entry's position in the array. ext.EntryFile is the first existing entry path. ext.RelTag is the dir relative to root (no .ts/.js strip — relTagForDir). ext.Kind == "package". When ALL entries are missing, classifyDir falls through to cases b/c/d/e unchanged (isExt=false, descend=true). This output is consumed by Index (index.go WalkDir callback) and indirectly by resolve, search, check, and ui.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the classifyDir doc comment in discover.go — the case (a) description (~lines 96-100) currently says 'EntryFile = first existing pi.extensions entry' which is the CORRECT spec but the code didn't match; update the inline comment at the fall-through (~line 146) to say 'no existing entry among ANY declared pi.extensions'. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.
6. TESTS (implicit TDD): Add TestClassifyDirPackageMultiEntryFirstMissing — package.json with pi.extensions ["./src/MISSING.ts", "./src/real.ts"], only real.ts exists. Assert isExt=true, Kind="package", EntryFile ends with src/real.ts, shouldDescend=false. Add TestClassifyDirPackageMultiEntryAllMissing — ["./a.ts", "./b.ts"], neither exists. Assert isExt=false, descend=true (extends existing TestClassifyDirPackageNonExistentEntry which only tests single-entry-missing). Add an end-to-end test via walkClassified or Index verifying the multi-entry package resolves as one entry, not fragmented stray files. Use the writeFile and writePackageJSON helpers from jsdoc_test.go/extension_test.go.

milestones.title: Discovery Walk Robustness (Issues 3, 4, 6)
milestones.description: Three independent fixes all in internal/discover/index.go (the Index function and its WalkDir callback). Issue 3: an index.ts at the store root collapses the entire store into one bogus '.' entry. Issue 4: a symlinked weave_EXTENSIONS_DIR passes --path but yields an empty catalog because WalkDir cannot descend a symlinked root. Issue 6: node_modules/, .git/, and hidden files pollute the catalog. All three MUST be implemented sequentially by a single agent (same file) — the subtask dependency chain enforces this.

tasks.title: Prevent root-level index.ts from collapsing the store (Issue 3)
tasks.description: The WalkDir callback in Index runs classifyDir on the walk root itself. If root contains index.ts, case (c) classifies the ROOT as a dir extension with relTag '.' (filepath.Rel(root, root)), then SkipDir prunes the ENTIRE store. Fix: skip classification when path == root — the root is always a container.

subtasks.title: Add root-skip guard in Index WalkDir callback
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/fix_design.md §Bug 3 and architecture/bug_cascade_map.md Bug 4 (the root-classification issue). discover.Index (internal/discover/index.go:57-68) WalkDir callback runs classifyDir(root, path) on EVERY directory including path == root. filepath.WalkDir ALWAYS visits root first as a directory entry. If root contains index.ts, classifyDir case (c) fires: relTagForDir(root, root) = filepath.Rel(root, root) = ".", then the callback returns filepath.SkipDir which at the ROOT level prunes the ENTIRE subtree.
2. INPUT: `root` (absolute extensions dir path, from filepath.Abs at index.go:44) and `path` (current WalkDir entry), both available in the WalkDir callback closure.
3. LOGIC: At the top of the `if d.IsDir()` branch (index.go:61), BEFORE calling classifyDir, add:
   ```go
   if path == root {
       return nil // root is the store container, never an extension — always descend
   }
   ```
   This is a 3-line guard. No mocking needed. The comparison `path == root` is exact because filepath.WalkDir passes the same root string it was given (after filepath.Abs cleaned it).
4. OUTPUT: Index no longer produces a '.' entry when root contains index.ts. All real extensions under root are discovered normally. An index.ts at root is simply ignored (not classified as an extension, not descended into as a category folder — it is a file entry that classifyFile rejects since its base name is index.ts).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add an inline comment in index.go Index() WalkDir callback explaining the root-skip rule ('the walk root is the store container, never an extension itself'). This rides WITH the work.
6. TESTS (implicit TDD): Add TestIndexRootIndexTSDoesNotCollapse — root has index.ts + sub/x.ts. Assert len(got)==1, got[0].RelTag=="sub/x", and there is NO entry with RelTag "." . Use t.TempDir() and writeFile helper from jsdoc_test.go.

tasks.title: Skip node_modules, .git, and hidden entries during walk (Issue 6)
tasks.description: WalkDir descends into node_modules/ and .git/, discovering their contents (npm packages with index.js) as extensions. Hidden files (.secret.ts) are also discovered. Add skip logic for well-known non-extension directories and hidden entries to prevent catalog pollution.

subtasks.title: Add skipDirs set and hidden-entry skip to WalkDir callback
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/fix_design.md §Bug 6. WalkDir descends into every directory including node_modules/ (created by npm install at the store root) and .git/. Hidden files (.secret.ts) pass isExtensionFile (they are *.ts, not index.*). index.go currently imports errors, io/fs, os, path/filepath, sort — it does NOT import 'strings'; you MUST add 'strings' to the import block.
2. INPUT: The WalkDir callback in Index() (internal/discover/index.go:57), as modified by P1.M2.T1.S1 (which added the root-skip guard). `d fs.DirEntry` is the current entry; `d.Name()` gives the base name; `d.IsDir()` distinguishes dirs from files.
3. LOGIC: Add a package-level `var skipDirs = map[string]bool{"node_modules": true, ".git": true}`. In the dir branch, AFTER the root-skip guard and BEFORE classifyDir, add:
   ```go
   name := d.Name()
   if skipDirs[name] || strings.HasPrefix(name, ".") {
       return filepath.SkipDir // prune node_modules, .git, hidden dirs
   }
   ```
   In the file branch, BEFORE classifyFile, add:
   ```go
   if strings.HasPrefix(d.Name(), ".") {
       return nil // skip hidden files (.secret.ts, etc.)
   }
   ```
   Add "strings" to the import block of index.go. No mocking needed.
4. OUTPUT: node_modules/, .git/, hidden directories (any dir whose base name starts with '.'), and hidden files (any file whose base name starts with '.') are excluded from the catalog. Non-hidden extensions under root/ and category folders are unaffected.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the Index doc comment (index.go:~line 30) to mention that well-known non-extension directories (node_modules, .git) and hidden entries are skipped during the walk. This rides WITH the work.
6. TESTS (implicit TDD): Add TestIndexSkipsNodeModules — root has myext.ts + node_modules/somepkg/index.js + node_modules/somepkg/package.json. Assert only 'myext' discovered. Add TestIndexSkipsHiddenFile — root has myext.ts + .secret.ts. Assert only 'myext'. Add TestIndexSkipsGitDir — root has myext.ts + .git/hooks/some.ts. Assert only 'myext'. Use writeFile helper.

tasks.title: Resolve symlinked EXTENSIONS_DIR before walking (Issue 4)
tasks.description: findEnv returns the symlink path verbatim (os.Stat follows it, filepath.Abs does not resolve it). filepath.WalkDir Lstats the root, sees ModeSymlink, and walks nothing — producing an empty catalog with exit 0 from --path but exit 1 from --list. Fix: EvalSymlinks the root in Index before walking.

subtasks.title: Add EvalSymlinks resolution for walk root in Index

milestones.title: Validation and Metadata Edge Cases (Issues 2, 5)
milestones.description: Two independent fixes in different files. Issue 2 (jsdoc.go): the degenerate empty JSDoc block /**/ extracts '/' as the description because the opener and closer overlap. Issue 5 (check.go): a package with all-missing pi.extensions entries is reported as an 'empty category folder' WARN instead of the §9 ERROR. Issue 5 depends on Issue 1 (M1) being fixed first, since the multi-entry fix changes what 'all missing' means.

tasks.title: Fix empty single-line JSDoc /**/ extraction (Issue 2)
tasks.description: ExtractJSDoc's single-line branch strips the /** opener before finding the */ closer. For /**/, the opener (pos 0-2) and closer (pos 2-3) overlap at pos 2 — stripping /** destroys the closer's *, leaving a stray '/'. Fix: find */ on the original line first, then extract the content between opener and closer positions.

subtasks.title: Fix ExtractJSDoc single-line branch for degenerate /**/ overlap

tasks.title: Surface §9 ERROR for all-missing pi.extensions in check (Issue 5)
tasks.description: After Issue 1's fix (M1), a package.json whose pi.extensions are ALL missing still falls through classifyDir to a plain dir, yields zero discover.Index entries, and check emits a misleading 'empty category folder' WARN (exit 0). The §9 requirement wants an ERROR naming the bad pi.extensions path (exit 1). Fix: in appendEmptyFolderFindings, detect a package.json with all-missing pi.extensions before the WARN and emit an ERROR instead.

subtasks.title: Add allMissingPiExtensions detection and ERROR in appendEmptyFolderFindings

milestones.title: Sync Changeset-Level Documentation
milestones.description: Final documentation sweep to ensure README.md and any overview docs accurately reflect the post-fix discovery behavior. Per SOW §5 Mode B: cross-cutting docs that only make sense once the whole changeset is in place ride as a final task with dependencies on all implementing subtasks.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Review README.md for accuracy against the post-fix behavior. The primary update is documenting that node_modules/, .git/, and hidden entries are skipped during discovery (Issue 6). Verify the package-kind description is consistent with the fixed classifyDir. No new doc files — update existing README.md content only.

subtasks.title: Update README.md to reflect post-fix discovery behavior
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/fix_design.md §Documentation plan. The README.md 'How extensions are organized' section accurately describes the three extension kinds. For Issue 1 (multi-entry packages), the README was ALREADY correct ('a package.json whose pi.extensions array names at least one existing entry') — the code was wrong, not the docs; no change needed there. For Issue 6 (skip node_modules/.git/hidden), the README does NOT currently mention any skip rules — this is the primary addition. Issues 2, 3, 4, 5 are internal fixes with no user-facing README impact beyond what Mode A doc comments already cover.
2. INPUT: README.md at project root. All completed subtask changes (P1.M1.T1.S1 through P1.M3.T2.S1). The post-fix behavior: classifyDir iterates all pi.extensions entries; Index skips root classification, node_modules/.git/hidden entries, and resolves symlinked roots; check reports ERROR for all-missing pi.extensions; ExtractJSDoc handles degenerate /**/.
3. LOGIC: Review README.md section by section:
   - 'How extensions are organized': Add a note (1-2 sentences) that `node_modules/`, `.git/`, and hidden files (names starting with `.`) are skipped during discovery. This can go after the 'Once a directory is recognized...' paragraph or in a new bullet under the extension-kinds list.
   - Verify the package kind description ('names at least one existing entry') matches the fixed classifyDir (it does — no change needed, but confirm).
   - Verify the 'How weave finds the store' section is consistent with the symlink fix (the env var rule now works with symlinked paths — the description is still accurate, optionally note that symlinks are followed).
   - Do NOT add documentation for Bugs 2, 3, 5 — these are internal edge-case fixes with no user-facing surface.
   - Do NOT create new doc files. Update existing README.md only.
4. OUTPUT: README.md reflects the post-fix discovery behavior. No stale or misleading claims. A reader knows that node_modules/.git/hidden are excluded from discovery.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B — changeset-level sweep per SOW §5). No further doc subtask needed.
